extra.message: User input from params["id"] is concatenated directly into a SQL SELECT query string passed to cursor.execute() without parameterized queries. An attacker can inject arbitrary SQL (e.g., UNION-based, boolean-based blind, time-based blind).

cwe: CWE-89: Improper Neutralization of Special Elements used in an SQL Command ('SQL Injection')

extra.message: User input from params["comment"] is interpolated via %s directly into a SQL INSERT statement. This allows SQL injection through the comment parameter, enabling data exfiltration or database manipulation.

cwe: CWE-89: Improper Neutralization of Special Elements used in an SQL Command ('SQL Injection')

extra.message: User input from params.get("password") is concatenated directly into a SQL WHERE clause in the login query. While the username is sanitized with re.sub(r"[^\w]", ...), the password field is not sanitized at all, allowing SQL injection (e.g., ' OR '1' LIKE '1 to bypass authentication).

cwe: CWE-89: Improper Neutralization of Special Elements used in an SQL Command ('SQL Injection')

extra.message: User input from params["v"] is inserted directly into HTML content via string formatting (r"\g<1>%s\g<2>" % params["v"]) without HTML escaping, allowing reflected cross-site scripting.

cwe: CWE-79: Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation ('Cross-site Scripting')

extra.message: Comments stored in the database (inserted via params["comment"] on line 50) are rendered into HTML table cells using "%s" % value without html.escape(). An attacker can inject persistent JavaScript by submitting a comment containing

cwe: CWE-79: Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation ('Cross-site Scripting')

extra.message: Results from SQL queries (cursor.fetchall()) are rendered into HTML table cells using "%s" % value without html.escape(). An attacker can use SQL injection to inject HTML/JavaScript that executes in the victim's browser.

cwe: CWE-79: Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation ('Cross-site Scripting')

extra.message: The HTML_PREFIX template contains client-side JavaScript that uses document.write() with untrusted data from document.location.hash (via decodeURIComponent). An attacker can craft a URL with a malicious hash fragment (e.g., #lang=) to execute arbitrary JavaScript in the victim's browser (DOM-based XSS).

cwe: CWE-79: Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation ('Cross-site Scripting')

extra.message: User input from params["callback"] is inserted directly into the JSONP response as a JavaScript function name without validation or sanitization (e.g., %s(" % params["callback"]). An attacker can inject arbitrary JavaScript by setting callback to something like alert(1)//, which results in alert(1)//({...}).

cwe: CWE-79: Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation ('Cross-site Scripting')

extra.message: User input from params["object"] is deserialized using pickle.loads(). Python pickle deserialization can execute arbitrary code during unpickling. An attacker can craft a malicious pickle payload to achieve remote code execution on the server.

cwe: CWE-502: Deserialization of Untrusted Data

extra.message: User input from params["path"] is passed to open() after os.path.abspath() when the input does not contain "://". os.path.abspath() does NOT prevent path traversal; it simply resolves the path. An attacker can read arbitrary files on the server using sequences like ../../etc/passwd to traverse directories.

cwe: CWE-22: Improper Limitation of a Pathname to a Restricted Directory ('Path Traversal')

extra.message: When params["path"] contains "://", it is passed directly to urllib.request.urlopen(), allowing Server-Side Request Forgery. An attacker can make the server fetch internal resources (e.g., http://169.254.169.254/latest/meta-data/ for cloud metadata, or http://localhost:internal-ports/ for internal services).

cwe: CWE-918: Server-Side Request Forgery (SSRF)

extra.message: User input from params["domain"] is concatenated into a shell command ("nslookup " + params["domain"]) and executed with shell=True via subprocess.run(). An attacker can inject arbitrary OS commands using shell metacharacters (e.g., ; rm -rf / or | cat /etc/passwd).

cwe: CWE-78: Improper Neutralization of Special Elements used in an OS Command ('OS Command Injection')

extra.message: The lxml XML parser is explicitly configured with load_dtd=True, resolve_entities=True, and no_network=False. This enables processing of external DTDs and resolution of external entities, allowing XML External Entity (XXE) attacks. An attacker can read arbitrary server files or make SSRF requests via crafted XML input.

cwe: CWE-611: Improper Restriction of XML External Entity Reference

extra.message: User input from params["name"] is interpolated directly into an XPath expression (".//user[name/text()='%s']" % params["name"]) without sanitization. An attacker can manipulate the XPath query to access unauthorized data (e.g., using ' or '1'='1 to return all users).

cwe: CWE-643: Improper Neutralization of Data within XPath Expressions ('XPath Injection')

extra.message: Results from XPath queries are rendered into HTML using "Surname: %s" without html.escape(). An attacker can use XPath injection to inject HTML/JavaScript that executes in the victim's browser.

cwe: CWE-79: Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation ('Cross-site Scripting')

extra.message: User input from params["size"] is converted to int and used to control the size of a string allocation ("#" * int(params["size"]) repeated int(params["size"]) times). There is no upper bound validation, allowing an attacker to cause denial of service by supplying a very large value that consumes excessive memory and CPU.

cwe: CWE-400: Uncontrolled Resource Consumption

extra.message: User input from params["include"] is used to read a file (local via open() or remote via urllib.request.urlopen()), and the content is then passed to exec(). This allows Remote Code Execution: an attacker can either include a local file containing Python code or fetch a remote URL serving malicious Python code, which is then executed on the server with exec(program, envs).

cwe: CWE-94: Improper Control of Generation of Code ('Code Injection')
CWE-98: Improper Control of Filename for Include/Require Statement ('PHP File Inclusion')

extra.message: User input from params["redir"] is inserted directly into a <meta http-equiv="refresh" content="0; url=%s"/> tag without validation. An attacker can craft a URL that redirects victims to a malicious site (e.g., /?redir=https://evil.com/phishing).

cwe: CWE-601: Redirection to an Untrusted Site ('Open Redirect')

extra.message: User input from params.get("charset") is inserted directly into the Content-Type HTTP header without validation or sanitization. An attacker can inject newline characters (\r\n) in the charset parameter to split the header and inject arbitrary HTTP response headers, enabling HTTP response splitting attacks.

cwe: CWE-113: Improper Neutralization of CRLF Sequences in HTTP Headers ('HTTP Response Splitting')

extra.message: Multiple user passwords (7en8aiDoh!, 12345, gandalf, phest1945) are hardcoded in the USERS_XML string within the source code. Hardcoded credentials cannot be rotated without modifying the source code and are visible to anyone with access to the repository.

cwe: CWE-798: Use of Hard-coded Credentials

extra.message: The X-XSS-Protection header is explicitly set to "0", disabling the browser's built-in XSS filter. This removes a defense-in-depth mechanism that could help mitigate reflected XSS attacks in older browsers.

cwe: CWE-16: Configuration
CWE-1004: Sensitive Cookie Without 'HttpOnly' Flag

extra.message: When an exception occurs, traceback.format_exc() is called and the full Python traceback (including file paths, line numbers, and code snippets) is returned to the client in the HTTP response. This leaks internal implementation details that can aid attackers in reconnaissance.

cwe: CWE-209: Generation of Error Message Containing Sensitive Information

extra.message: The do_GET handler processes all requests without any authentication or authorization checks. Sensitive operations (file reading, command execution, code execution, database queries) are accessible to any client that can reach the server. The SESSIONID cookie set on line 68 is never validated.

cwe: CWE-306: Missing Authentication for Critical Function
CWE-862: Missing Authorization
